name: NOVOGOLF
about: NOVOGOLF is a premium golf fitting and building facility with locations in Tappan, NY and Closter, NJ. The venue specializes in custom club fittings, club building, and technology-based golf analysis. NOVOGOLF utilizes high-end Trackman technology for swing and ball flight analysis, offering a comprehensive indoor-to-outdoor fitting experience in its Technology Center and a 300+ yard range. The facility caters to golfers of all levels, from novices to touring professionals, with highly trained fitters and club technicians who provide personalized services.
why_golfers_like_it: Industry-leading Trackman technology for precise swing and ball flight analysis
Outdoor fitting sessions allowing golfers to see true ball flight on a 300+ yard range
Highly trained fitters and technicians with PGA and LPGA tour experience

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food services available on-site.
No mention of alcohol services available on-site.

simulator_hardware.notes: Official choice of the PGA Tour and The R&A for swing and ball flight analysis, used in outdoor fittings.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: No, NOVOGOLF provides all necessary fitting clubs and shafts during your session. However, if you're interested in building custom clubs, your specifications can be used to create a set tailored to you.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention specific policies regarding outside food and drinks, suggesting there may not be on-site dining options. It's best to contact NOVOGOLF directly for confirmation.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulation take?
faq.answer: The website does not provide specific timing for simulated 18-hole rounds. Fitting sessions are described as detailed and in-depth, with pricing separate from equipment purchase.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: NOVOGOLF does not explicitly mention kid-friendly features or services in its online content. The facility's focus on high-end fittings and technology suggests it is geared more toward serious golfers.
